name: Ownership & Borrowing
def: A compile-time discipline where each value has one owner and references are checked for validity.
problem: Guarantee memory and thread safety without a garbage collector or runtime checks.
mechanism: Each value has a single owner; it is moved or borrowed (shared & or exclusive &mut). The borrow checker rejects aliasing+mutation and references outliving their data.
benefits: Memory safety with zero runtime cost
failures: Reaching for Rc<RefCell> / unsafe to escape the checker
whenNot: Rapid prototyping where GC friction is lower
myths: "Borrow checker = runtime checks" — it is entirely compile-time
